data.title: Regeneron reports Q2 earnings of $12.89/share, beating consensus, with sales of $3.68B, up 4% YoY. Eylea sales decrease 25% YoY to $1.15B.
data.description: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals Inc. reported adjusted earnings of $12.89 per share in the second quarter, showing a 12% increase year-over-year and surpassing the consensus of $8.57. The company's sales totaled $3.68 billion, marking a 4% increase year-over-year and exceeding the consensus of $3.29 billion. U.S. net sales for Eylea HD and Eylea dropped by 25% year-over-year to $1.15 billion, with $393 million from Eylea HD and $754 million from Eylea. Net product sales of Eylea HD rose in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the same period in 2024 due to increased demand. Conversely, net product sales of EYLEA were negatively affected by lower sales volumes attributed to competitive pressures and a shift in market share to compounded bevacizumab because of patient affordability issues and transitioning patients to EYLEA HD.

data.title: Pheton Holdings Ltd in China addresses market activity and rumors, reassures shareholders amid speculation.
data.description: Pheton Holdings Ltd, a leading healthcare solution provider based in Beijing, China, released a statement on August 1, 2025, addressing the recent market fluctuations and concerns raised by a third-party publication. The company specializes in treatment planning systems for brachytherapy and related products. In response to shareholder unease, Pheton emphasized its commitment to transparency and integrity.

data.title: Invisors named 27th among 350 ranked companies in UK's Best Workplaces for Women™ 2025 List.
data.description: In Glasgow, Scotland, the 2025 UK's Best Workplaces for Women™ has announced Invisors as the 27th best company in the Medium company category out of 350 organisations. Invisors focuses on incorporating diverse perspectives in their work environment.

data.title: Stem cells from human gum tissue could boost oral tissue regeneration, says Penn Dental study.
data.description: Researchers at Penn Dental Medicine have discovered that the molecular secretions from human gum tissue stem cells may enhance the healing and regeneration of damaged mouth tissues. The study, conducted in August 2025, suggests the potential use of these stem cells to accelerate tissue healing in the mouth. The findings provide insight into possible future applications of stem cell therapy in dentistry. This breakthrough research marks a significant step towards utilizing human gum tissue stem cells for tissue regeneration and healing, with the aim of improving oral health.

data.title: BEYOND unveils PASSO on Palm Jumeirah in Dubai, a striking sculpture adding to the city's skyline.
data.description: In Dubai, United Arab Emirates, BEYOND has unveiled PASSO, a striking new addition to Palm Jumeirah. This sculptural icon promises to captivate with its unique design and presence in the area.

data.title: Metaplanet to raise $3.7B through stock for Bitcoin spree, introducing perpetual preferred shares with 6% dividends for acquisitions.
data.description: A new stock offering is set to introduce perpetual preferred shares with dividends of up to 6%, with all funds designated for the acquisition of BTC.

data.title: US labor market shows a hiring slowdown - July saw only 73K new jobs, below expectations. April and June revised jobs slashed. Trump considers Fed takeover.
data.description: The U.S. labor market disappointed in July as the official jobs report showed a sharp hiring slowdown, with nonfarm payrolls rising by only 73,000 compared to the expected 110,000. Private sector added 83,000 jobs while government employment declined by 10,000. May and June saw significant downward revisions in job growth numbers, with May revised from 144,000 to 19,000, and June from 147,000 to 14,000. This made both months the worst for job growth since December 2020, wiping out a total of 258,000 jobs from earlier estimates. Wages, however, remained stable. This news reflects the impact of economic uncertainty from Trump's tariffs, with investors and the White House receiving an unwelcome reality check.
